opp.name: American Place - eProc Opp - April '26
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma calls recorded. SF shows Wilson Geraldo met Mike McCarthy at Chicago Roundtable; American Place has a project in place with RFP expected soon.
• Zero AI cues in description or Next Step beyond 'RFP expected soon'. No reference to Merlin, agentic, autonomous procurement, or any AI use case.
• Created mid-April 2026, last activity early May. Too early to assess - default lower curious band until the RFP arrives and reveals AI requirements.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business value, savings target, cost reduction, or operational metrics have been captured. The Salesforce opportunity record carries no Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, or Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c content. No buyer calls were discovered (opp + account + attendee-email Avoma pulls returned zero matched meetings), so no pain or ROI conversation has been transcribed. The only signal is the product scope (eProcurement + Merlin), which implies procurement process improvement and AI-driven negotiation, but the buyer has not articulated why, the cost of inaction, or the targeted impact. Without a quantified value case this deal cannot credibly advance past RFP evaluation or secure EB sign-off. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with Wilson Giraldo (and ideally his finance or operations stakeholder) to surface the business problem, quantify current-state inefficiency (spend under management, cycle time, maverick spend, supplier base size), and build a dollar-anchored ROI model before the RFP is issued.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c field is empty, and the single OpportunityContactRole is Wilson Giraldo (Procurement & Supplier Diversity Manager) with role = Evaluator, not Champion. A champion would have access to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, advocate for Zycus internally, share the buyer's evaluation criteria and competitive landscape before the RFP is public, and drive the agenda to favor Zycus's Merlin AI differentiation. Wilson Giraldo may become a champion, but no evidence of advocacy, multi-threading support, or insider guidance has been captured (no calls, no documented introductions, no shared internal insights). The deal is single-threaded and has been silent for 30 days, which suggests Wilson is either not championing or not empowered to move the deal forward alone. The move is to re-engage Wilson Giraldo within 7 days, assess his willingness and ability to champion Zycus (Does he believe Merlin is the right answer? Will he introduce us to procurement leadership, finance, or the EB? Will he share the RFP criteria before it is issued?), and if he is willing, ask him to introduce Zycus to the decision-making stakeholders (his manager, the VP Procurement, or the CFO/finance lead who will approve the budget) so we can build a multi-threaded relationship and develop a champion closer to power.

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is entirely unknown. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty (marked 'unknown' in ground truth), and no Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, or competitive-intelligence content exists in the opportunity record. No Avoma buyer calls were discovered, so no competitive conversation (who else is being evaluated, what the buyer likes/dislikes about alternatives, pricing benchmarks, feature gaps) has been captured. The Next_Step__c field states 'RFP expected soon', which strongly implies a multi-vendor evaluation is planned (RFPs are issued to compare options), but which eProcurement platforms the buyer is considering (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, Zip, or a Merlin-capable peer like Keelvar or Icertis for contract intelligence) is unknown. The deal is at risk of being treated as a commodity eProcurement RFP where Merlin's agentic AI negotiation and contract intelligence differentiation is not understood or scored, and Zycus could lose on price or incumbent inertia without knowing it. The move is to ask Wilson Giraldo on the next call (within 7 days) who else is being considered, what alternatives or incumbents they are replacing or comparing against, and what the shortlist looks like — then build a competitive wedge that positions Merlin's AI-first sourcing and negotiation agent as the separator from traditional eProcurement workflow platforms.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. The Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are all empty in Salesforce. No Avoma buyer calls were discovered, so no pain conversation has been captured. The only signal is the product scope (eProcurement + Merlin), which implies the buyer wants to improve procurement process efficiency and leverage AI for sourcing/negotiation, but the WHY is missing: What is broken today? What is the cost of the current state (maverick spend, cycle time, supplier risk, lack of spend visibility, manual contract negotiation)? What happens if they do nothing? Who owns the pain (procurement, finance, operations)? Without confirmed pain and a named pain owner, this deal has no internal urgency and will drift. The next step field says 'RFP expected soon', which suggests the buyer believes they have a problem worth solving (or they would not be issuing an RFP), but Zycus has not captured or validated it. The move is to run a pain-discovery call with Wilson Giraldo (and ideally his manager or the finance/operations stakeholder who chartered this evaluation) within 7 days to surface the specific pain (e.g. 'we have $50M in tail spend with no visibility and 90-day sourcing cycles that delay projects'), quantify it, and identify the pain owner who will champion the business case internally.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and implementation scoping are entirely unknown. No Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c, or procurement-process fields are populated in Salesforce. No buyer calls exist in Avoma, so the paper process (MSA vs standard terms, SOW requirements, InfoSec questionnaire, data residency/privacy review, ERP/AP system integration scope, whether an SI or partner will be involved in implementation) has never been discussed. For a $100k eProcurement + Merlin SaaS deal the buyer will likely require an InfoSec review (data access, supplier data handling, AI model transparency), legal redlines on the MSA, and clarity on integration with their existing ERP (SAP, Oracle, Workday, etc.) and AP automation stack. The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field is empty, so it is unclear whether this is a greenfield eProcurement implementation, a replacement of an incumbent (and if so, when that contract expires and whether it creates a forcing function), or an add-on to an existing procurement suite. The move is to surface the paper-process requirements in the first discovery or RFP kickoff call: ask Wilson Giraldo what the procurement, legal, and InfoSec review steps are, what integration and implementation scoping is required, and whether there is an incumbent contract expiration date that anchors the timeline.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. The Salesforce X10a_Sponsor__c and Who_will_approve_budget__c fields are empty, and no contact role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists on this opportunity. The single buyer-side contact is Wilson Giraldo, titled Procurement & Supplier Diversity Manager — a process owner and evaluator, not a budget authority. No Avoma calls were discovered, so no pricing, budget, or approval-chain conversation has been captured. For a $100k eProcurement + Merlin deal the EB is likely a VP Finance, CFO, or VP Operations who controls the procurement technology budget and would sign off on a six-figure SaaS investment. The deal is 180 days from close but still in Qualified with no EB mapped, which means budget authority, approval process, and commercial negotiation readiness are all unconfirmed. The move is to ask Wilson Giraldo directly (on the next call) who will approve the budget for this procurement platform investment, map the approval chain, and secure an introduction to that person within 14 days.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process, timeline, and approval chain are unconfirmed. The What_is_the_decision_process__c and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c fields are empty. The Next_Step__c field states 'RFP expected soon', implying the buyer intends to run a formal RFP round, but the process after that (shortlisting, demos, use-case validation, commercial negotiation, contract approval, legal/InfoSec review, implementation scoping) has not been mapped. No Avoma calls exist, so the buyer has not walked through their internal evaluation stages or named who participates at each gate. The single contact (Wilson Giraldo, Procurement Manager) suggests he may be driving the RFP, but whether he owns the full vendor selection or reports findings to a steering committee, finance, or operations leadership is unknown. The close date is 180 days out (23 Dec 2026), but without knowing the process milestones and approval layers it is impossible to validate that timeline. The move is to ask Wilson Giraldo on the next call to describe the full decision process from RFP issuance through contract signature: who evaluates, who shortlists, who runs demos, who negotiates pricing, who signs, and what the target date is for each gate — then build a mutual close plan that maps Zycus's deliverables to those gates.

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria, evaluation framework, or RFP requirements have been documented. The Salesforce Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields are empty. The Next_Step__c field states 'RFP expected soon' (as of the last activity 27 May, 30 days ago), but no RFP has been received and no pre-RFP discovery or criteria validation session has been logged. No buyer calls exist in Avoma, so the criteria the buyer will use to score vendors (functional fit, AI capability, integration with their ERP/AP systems, pricing model, implementation timeline, supplier diversity support) are unknown. The next step field implies the buyer is preparing an RFP, which typically codifies the criteria, but Zycus has not influenced or previewed it. The risk is a commodity RFP that underweights Merlin's AI negotiation differentiation or includes criteria Zycus cannot meet. The move is to request a pre-RFP alignment call with Wilson Giraldo (and ideally procurement leadership) within 7 days to understand their evaluation priorities, surface where Merlin's agentic sourcing and contract intelligence create separation from traditional eProcurement platforms, and shape the RFP scoring before it is issued.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling. No buyer engagement for 30 days since 27 May, no buyer calls discovered in Avoma, single-threaded through one Procurement Manager with no multi-threading or power access, and an overdue RFP that has not materialized. The deal is silent and stalled, waiting on the buyer with no visibility into whether the evaluation is still active or has been paused/deprioritized.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Apr 2026, 78 days in Qualified stage with no submission-date movement. Last Salesforce activity 27 May (30 days ago). Close date 23 Dec 2026 = 180 days out. No forward slip logged, but at current pace (78 days, no RFP issued, no multi-threading, no EB/DM mapped) the deal cannot credibly reach Shortlisted + Vendor Selected + Negotiation + Contract in 180 days. Forecast Pipeline is appropriate given thinness, but the single-thread and 30-day silence signal a stall.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage $100k deal remains stalled in Qualified for 78 days with zero buyer engagement for 30 days. Single-threaded to Procurement Manager, no EB/champion mapped, and promised RFP has not materialized. Weak foundation threatens viability.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with zero discovery depth, single-threaded, no EB/DM/champion, no competitive intelligence, and silent for 30 days waiting on an overdue RFP. The highest-leverage moves are to restart buyer engagement immediately, run deep discovery to surface pain/ROI/criteria, multi-thread to power, and shape the RFP before it is issued as a commodity evaluation.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape unknown. No competitors named in Salesforce (Competitors__c empty), no competitive-intelligence fields populated, and no buyer calls discovered in Avoma. The Next_Step__c field ('RFP expected soon') implies a multi-vendor evaluation is planned, but the shortlist, incumbents, and alternatives being considered are unconfirmed. The deal is at risk of a commodity eProcurement RFP where Merlin's AI differentiation is not understood or scored. No current threat can be named because no competitor has been identified.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning unknown. Products field includes 'Merlin' (Zycus's AI negotiation and sourcing agent), but no AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c data exists in Salesforce to indicate buyer AI appetite or engagement with the Merlin narrative. No buyer calls discovered in Avoma, so no AI conversation (how Merlin's agentic negotiation, contract intelligence, or supplier analytics differentiate from traditional eProcurement workflow platforms, or whether the buyer values AI-driven sourcing) has been captured. The RFP (when issued) may not score AI capability, and Zycus risks being evaluated as a generic eProcurement vendor rather than an AI-first platform. Cannot assess positioning strength without knowing whether the buyer understands or values Merlin's AI differentiation.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations and evaluation criteria are unknown. No discovery calls, no RFP preview, no business requirements, and no pain or decision-criteria conversation has been captured. The product scope (eProcurement + Merlin) implies the buyer wants procurement process automation and AI-driven sourcing/negotiation, but their specific priorities (cost savings, cycle time, spend visibility, supplier diversity support, ERP integration, AI transparency) and how they will score vendors are unconfirmed. Cannot assess fit without knowing what they are looking for.

value.flag: RE-ENGAGE NOW (priority 1): Deal has been silent for 30 days since 27 May with no buyer engagement and an overdue RFP. The immediate action is for Bailey Erazo to reach out to Wilson Giraldo within 2 business days (by 30 Jun) to restart the conversation, confirm the RFP timeline, and secure a discovery call. A 30-day silence in Qualified stage with 180 days to close signals the buyer may be pausing, reprioritizing, or running a quiet evaluation without Zycus visibility. Without re-engagement in the next week this deal will go dark.

value.flag: MULTI-THREAD TO the relevant stakeholder (priority 2): This deal is dangerously single-threaded through Wilson Giraldo (Procurement Manager, an evaluator with no budget authority) with no access to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement/finance leadership. For a $100k eProcurement + Merlin investment the EB is likely a VP Finance, CFO, or VP Operations, and the DM is likely VP Procurement or a senior procurement director. The move is to ask Wilson on the re-engagement call (within 7 days) to introduce Zycus to the budget owner and the procurement leader who will make the vendor decision, then use that introduction to build a champion closer to power and map the full buying committee. Without multi-threading Zycus cannot influence the decision criteria, validate budget, or secure executive sponsorship, and the deal will stall or lose in an RFP blind evaluation.

value.flag: SHAPE THE RFP BEFORE IT IS ISSUED (priority 3): The Next_Step__c field says 'RFP expected soon' (as of 27 May, now 30 days overdue), but Zycus has not previewed the RFP, influenced the evaluation criteria, or positioned Merlin's AI negotiation and contract intelligence differentiation. The risk is a commodity eProcurement RFP that scores traditional workflow and catalog features (where Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer are well-known) and underweights or ignores Merlin's agentic sourcing, supplier negotiation, and contract analytics capabilities. The move is to request a pre-RFP alignment session with Wilson Giraldo and procurement leadership (within 7 days, before the RFP is finalized) to understand their evaluation priorities, surface where AI-driven negotiation and spend intelligence create measurable ROI (cycle time reduction, cost avoidance, contract risk mitigation), and ensure the RFP scoring framework favors Zycus's differentiation. If the RFP is issued without Zycus influence, the deal becomes a feature-checklist comparison where Merlin's value is invisible.
